items.title: Fuse Feature Flag Systems for Enhanced UI Control in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update merges two independent feature-flag systems into a single function, `isFeatureEnabled`, allowing for more dynamic UI behavior based on backend configurations. The new implementation ensures that flags from both URL cookies and server responses are evaluated, enabling immediate updates to the UI without manual reloads. This change preserves existing behaviors while enhancing the flexibility of feature rollouts.

items.title: Fix granted tab access issue in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a permissions error when accessing the 'Authorized to me' tab in the applications console. The query parameters have been adjusted to use `user_id` and `affiliation` instead of the obsolete `role`, ensuring that users can retrieve their granted applications correctly. The fix maintains existing UI behavior while aligning with the new backend contract.

items.title: Revamped Token Management for Clarity in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Applications page by redesigning the token and top-up interface based on user feedback. Key changes include a full-width General Balance card, prominent action buttons for Keys, Usage, and Top Up, and a new section header for Dedicated Instances. These improvements aim to streamline user experience and make essential features more accessible.

items.title: Defer x402 Settlement Until Real Usage Records Arrive in Gateway ⚡
items.summary: Updated the x402 /record flow to detect x-usage-exempt placeholder records and skip settlement until the worker submits the delayed usage report. This preserves settlement context instead of consuming it on a zero-amount placeholder request. The change aligns x402 behavior with the existing non-x402 placeholder handling and restores correct metered billing and on-chain settlement for server-side usage reporting.

items.title: Corrected Sora video resolution labels and verified CLI connections in Backend ⚙️
items.summary: This update removes the incorrect 1080p label from Sora, replacing it with '高清' to match the actual maximum schema resolution of 1792x1024. All tested CLIs (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode) successfully connected using valid tokens, and protocol handshakes and tool manifests across 11 servers were validated. The change ensures that users see accurate resolution options and that tool configurations are fully functional.

items.title: Fixed Permit2 Witness Type Mismatch to Restore Successful Settlements in x402 ✅
items.summary: Updated the Permit2 typed-data builder to use the on-chain Witness struct name instead of X402Witness, resolving InvalidSigner() reverts caused by an EIP-712 typehash mismatch. The settle() ABI was also corrected to remove an unsupported bool return value. Validation included passing test coverage, successful mainnet simulation, and a confirmed Base mainnet settlement transaction using the patched signing flow.

items.title: Enhance EVM Signer and Fix Permit2 Witness in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an upto-scheme EVM signer and an `approve-permit2` CLI for Base/USDC. A critical fix renames the Permit2 witness struct to ensure on-chain signature acceptance, resolving previous verification issues. The changes have been tested successfully with real on-chain transactions, confirming their effectiveness.

items.title: Restore Authorize Entry for Applications in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the per-row Authorize entry for the new credauth flow that was unintentionally removed in a previous PR. Users can now see the 'Add authorization' button in the applications table, allowing them to mint grants directly from the UI. The feature is gated behind a URL flag for a controlled rollout, ensuring a smooth user experience.

items.title: Fixed Codex MCP Authentication Docs in Backend 🔧
items.summary: Updated Codex CLI documentation to use the correct MCP authentication configuration by replacing unsupported header subtables with the official http_headers format. The change affects 12 Codex-specific guides and ensures Authorization headers are actually sent when connecting to remote MCP servers. Other supported client configurations were revalidated against official documentation and required no changes.

items.title: Enhance Credential Filtering with Granted Parameter in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new query-string filter `?granted=true|false` on the `/credentials/` endpoint, allowing the Authorize page to request only relevant grants from the server. The implementation mirrors the `CredentialDetailSerializer.granted` logic, improving efficiency by reducing unnecessary data transfer. The change ensures that users can easily filter credentials based on their ownership status without client-side processing.

items.title: Update Client Guides with Accurate MCP Tool Names in Backend 🤖
items.summary: This update corrects fabricated names and capabilities of MCP tools across all client guides, impacting 38 files. The changes ensure that all tool names now accurately reflect the functionalities available, enhancing clarity for users. A thorough cross-check was conducted to validate the information, leading to the removal of inaccuracies and the restoration of essential details.

items.title: Enhance Cost Rules with Per-Model Max Credits in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces per-model max credits for various token-metered rules, allowing for more precise cost management. A total of 91 rules have been patched to include these new ceilings, enhancing the efficiency of cost calculations across multiple models. The implementation ensures that users will not pay more than their actual consumption, maintaining trust in the system.

items.title: Unify COS client for share-images uploads in Backend ⚡
items.summary: This PR extracts the COS client singleton into `app/utils/cos_client.py` and updates `upload_cos.py` and `files.py` to use it. The share-images upload path now inherits accelerate endpoint, connection pooling, timeout, MAZ-compatible storage class, and CDN configuration from the shared client. Legacy hard-coded settings are removed, improving reliability and reducing latency for cross-region uploads. No behavior changes occur for the existing `/api/v1/files/` endpoint.

items.title: Cleaned up MCP docs and fixed capability claims in Backend ⚙️
items.summary: This update removes inaccurate capability claims across multiple MCP services after verification with actual server responses. Veo audio features, ShortURL UTM tracking, and Sora resolution claims were corrected or removed. Verified true capabilities, like Seedance audio and Flux 6 models, remain documented. The changes ensure documentation accurately reflects real functionality and improves reliability for developers using PlatformBackend.

items.title: Standardized MCP Article Titles Across 118 Docs in Backend ✨
items.summary: This update rewrites the H1 titles in 118 zh-CN MCP integration articles, replacing clickbait phrasing with the standardized format `<Client> 对接 <Service> MCP`. The changes align in-article headings with sidebar titles already stored in the database. Only the H1 lines were modified; no other content was touched. After merging, running the sync script propagates updates to translation tables and 17 other locales.

items.title: Wire X402 facilitator addresses into production env in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This PR updates the production deployment manifest to include X402_BASE_FACILITATOR_ADDRESS and X402_SKALE_FACILITATOR_ADDRESS environment variables. Both values are sourced from the existing x402 secret and marked optional to prevent rollout issues if missing. With these changes, metered endpoints on the upto branch will correctly use facilitator addresses once the secrets are populated. There is no immediate behavior change until the secrets are set and the platform-gateway is restarted.

items.title: Standardize worktree locations and clean up legacy directories in Index ⚡
items.summary: This update enforces a single canonical location for all Git worktrees under `.worktrees/` at the monorepo root. Legacy sibling worktrees have been migrated or removed, ensuring no top-level `*-wt-*` directories remain. Preflight and cleanup scripts were updated to reflect the new structure, and explicit rules prevent creating worktrees outside the designated folder. The change streamlines repository organization and reduces accidental commits of temporary directories.

items.title: Add Studio Platform link to console sidebar in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: Introduces a new external link to Studio Platform below the User Profile in the console sidebar. The menu label has been added across all supported locale files to ensure full i18n coverage. Changes affect navigation and enhance quick access to Studio Platform for users.

items.title: Enhance balance handling with error isolation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the balance processing by implementing direct parsing for each provider and isolating errors to prevent one provider's failure from affecting others. Key changes include removing unnecessary code, fixing the balance retrieval for piapi, and ensuring all providers log their status without crashing the CronJob. The overall reliability of the balance monitoring system is significantly enhanced.

items.title: Corrected MCP Tool References Across Claude Code Guides in Backend 🛠️
items.summary: Updated the Claude Code + MCP documentation series to replace nonexistent or incorrect MCP tool names with verified tool names from live MCP servers. The changes cover Luma, Veo, Midjourney, Seedream, Seedance, Sora, and Serp guides, including missing image-to-video and image-editing tools where applicable. The overview guide also adds MCP scope guidance to prevent configuration mistakes, and all referenced tools were validated against actual connected servers.

items.title: Exciting New Upto-Scheme Features in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Python client for the `upto` scheme, allowing users to utilize new payment methods advertised by the PlatformGateway. Key additions include the `sign_evm_upto_payment()` function and a CLI command for `approve-permit2`, enhancing payment flexibility. The implementation ensures seamless integration with existing systems and prepares for future phases of the rollout.

items.title: Application Pickers Show the Right Apps Again in Nexior 🔧
items.summary: Fixed application picker behavior across Nexior by restoring the GLOBAL scope filter for cross-service listings, preventing service-specific apps from appearing where they do not belong. Application queries were also migrated to the new user_id and affiliation contract, replacing deprecated role-based parameters. All service pickers now explicitly request both owned and granted applications, ensuring shared apps are surfaced consistently while aligning frontend behavior with the updated backend API.

items.title: Align application filters with new user_id + affiliation API in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: Updated the application query interface to replace the old `roles` parameter with `user_id` and `affiliation`, supporting multiple users and explicit access scopes. Modified the granted-apps tab to fetch applications using the new filter format. These changes ensure the frontend matches the updated PlatformBackend API, removing implicit server-side logic and making access queries explicit.

items.title: Refactor application filters for clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a two-axis filtering system for the applications API, replacing the previous `?roles=` parameter. The new parameters `?user_id` and `?affiliation` allow for clearer and more explicit filtering, enhancing user experience and security. Additionally, comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the functionality works as intended and documentation has been updated accordingly.

items.title: Enhance Metered API Support with Upto Scheme in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `upto` scheme for metered APIs in the gateway, allowing clients to set a ceiling on costs at the authentication stage. Key changes include new environment variables for facilitator addresses, modifications to payment requirement builders, and the addition of a helper function to retrieve maximum credits. This enhancement ensures more accurate billing for variable-cost APIs while maintaining existing functionality for fixed-cost APIs.

items.title: Streamline Application Role Filtering in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the overlapping query parameters for application role filtering into a single `roles=` parameter, simplifying the API usage. The previous `role` and `include_granted` parameters have been replaced, enhancing clarity and consistency with existing serialization methods. All related tests have been updated and successfully passed to ensure functionality remains intact.

items.title: Update Beachball to 2.65.5 with improved packing defaults in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR upgrades the Beachball dependency from version 2.65.4 to 2.65.5. Key changes include setting the default `packStyle` to 'layer', writing `versions.json` for layer-style packing, adding a link to change type help, and supporting fetch with `--no-tags`. These updates streamline package management and improve developer workflow. Package files `package.json` and `package-lock.json` were modified accordingly.

items.title: Introducing EVM Upto Scheme Handler for Metered Chat Billing in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update implements the new `upto` scheme for EVM chains, allowing chat APIs to manage payment ceilings effectively. Key features include the introduction of the `UptoEvmHandler`, which validates payment authorizations and settles actual usage amounts on-chain. The PR also includes new constants, migration for database fields, and comprehensive unit tests to ensure functionality.
